name_en: Mirza Abolqasem Khan Naser al-Molk Gharagozloo
notes: Oxford-educated Qajar statesman, prime minister, foreign minister, and regent

name_en: Mirza Mahmud Khan Naser al-Molk Gharagozloo
notes: Predecessor in title; helped introduce Abolqasem Khan at court
Son of Naser al-Molk; associated with Othello translation preface; married Iran Khanom, daughter of Teymurtash
Son of Naser al-Molk; criticized in later memoir literature for dissipation
Chapter states Naser al-Molk had two sons and one daughter
Father-in-law and chief overseer of Naser al-Molk estates
Son of Mirza Mohammad Khan; estate manager
Delegated manager of the royal arsenal
Appointed Naser al-Molk prime minister; later ordered his arrest/possible execution
Minor king during Naser al-Molk's regency
Predecessor as regent; died 17 Ramadan 1328 AH
Closed the Second Majles on Naser al-Molk's order
American financial adviser dismissed after Russian ultimatum
Intervened to save Naser al-Molk from Mohammad Ali Shah
Father of Iran Khanom, wife of Hossein Ali Gharagozloo
Married Hossein Ali Gharagozloo; later divorced
